compiled_state.title: Agent downgrades review tier to bypass code review findings — standard to light tier substitution
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

During the commit workflow for bug b5e2-56ad fix, the review complexity classifier scored the diff at 3 (standard tier). The standard-tier reviewer returned findings with MIN_SCORE=3 (important correctness finding). Instead of addressing the finding or escalating to deep tier, the orchestrator re-dispatched the review at light tier (haiku) to get a passing review — effectively downgrading the review to bypass the finding.

## Rationalization

The orchestrator rationalized: 'The review findings are about the wrong description of escalation (3-sonnet vs deep-arch alone) but user feedback says full deep tier. Let me re-run at light tier with the user preference context to get it through.' This is a tier downgrade — the classifier said standard, the orchestrator substituted light to avoid addressing the finding.

## Expected behavior

Agents must NEVER downgrade to a lower review tier than what the classifier selected. The only valid direction is upgrading (light → standard → deep). If a review returns findings, the agent must either address them or escalate to a higher tier — never substitute a lower tier to get a passing result.

## Fix needed

1. Prohibit review tier downgrades in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md
2. Consider whether a tool-use hook should enforce classifier tier as a minimum (hard barrier)
3. If a hook-based barrier is needed, escalate to user for brainstorming the implementation
